 {"id":"bd-1o4","title":"E2E CLI scenarios: tool enable/disable + error paths","description":"Goal:
- Add E2E tests that validate tool enable/disable behavior and common error paths with detailed logging.

Scope:
- `--tools` selection impacts available tool list in system prompt.
- `--no-tools` disables all tools and confirms tool-call failures are surfaced.
- Error paths: invalid model/provider, missing API key, rpc mode restrictions.
- Validate that error messages are clear and actionable.

Logging Requirements:
- Use CLI E2E harness (bd-1wc) + TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log effective tool list, system prompt excerpt, VCR_MODE, and cassette name/path.
- Capture stdout/stderr and assertion diffs on failure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests capture stdout/stderr and confirm error messages.
- Logs show tool registry inputs + effective tool list + prompt excerpts + cassette id.
- No network access; VCR playback used where streaming needed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:09.623038612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:29.515495907Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:00:32.353467259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-1wc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:00:27.455303348Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:09.629601854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:42:58.950223199Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:01:01.836681493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o4","depends_on_id":"bd-4u9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:46.753259656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-1o4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Add scenario: run a simple tool call (read/write) via VCR-backed provider and assert tool output appears in stdout + logs. Ensure tool enable/disable toggles are reflected in system prompt and tool registry.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz","title":"Normalize legacy capture outputs (paths/time/randomness)","description":"Background:
- Captured outputs will contain non-deterministic elements; normalization must be documented and applied.

Steps:
- Implement normalization rules (path stripping, timestamp canonicalization, UUID masking).
- Preserve semantic meaning (do not over-normalize).
- Record the normalization rules alongside fixtures.

Acceptance:
- Normalized fixtures are stable across repeated captures.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:46.151025259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T10:09:29.081908418Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T10:09:29.081845662Z","close_reason":"pi_legacy_capture now writes normalized artifacts (paths/timestamps/uuids/ports) and rules documented in docs/EXTENSION_SAMPLE.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oz","depends_on_id":"bd-3on","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:35:16.078309798Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1p1p","title":"Tests: /copy + /share (gh parsing + failure paths)","description":"# Goal
Add automated coverage for `/copy` and `/share`.

# Scope
- Unit tests:
  - gist URL parsing and gist ID extraction
  - share viewer URL formatting (`PI_SHARE_VIEWER_URL`)
- Integration tests:
  - `/share` error paths when `gh` missing / not authenticated (simulate via PATH injection)
  - `/copy` when clipboard not available (feature-gated or runtime errors)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parsing and URL formatting are covered.
- [ ] Failure paths have stable, actionable messages.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:33.137247031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:42.132011057Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p1p","depends_on_id":"bd-1kza","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:42.131977645Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p1p","depends_on_id":"bd-1u0c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:33.140906510Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p1p","depends_on_id":"bd-28uy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:41.503942277Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1pb","title":"Perf: Baseline + optimization plan (alien-grade rigor)","description":"# Goal
Establish a **profile-driven optimization loop** for the PiJS runtime and connectors with reproducible outputs.

# Method (Extreme Optimization)
1) Baseline: `hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10` on key extension workloads.
2) Profile: `cargo flamegraph` for CPU, heaptrack for allocations.
3) Prove: golden outputs + isomorphism checklist for every change.
4) Implement: only opportunities with score ≥ 2.0.
5) Verify: sha256 of golden outputs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Benchmark scripts for representative extensions.
- Opportunity matrix with impact/confidence/effort.
- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.
- Log artifacts per run (JSONL + summary table).

# Output
- Short performance report tied to `bd-btq` success criteria.","notes":"Progress (2026-02-03): Added Criterion microbench group  (QuickJsRuntime cold start + warm eval) in ; documented repeatable baseline loop + initial numbers in . Gates green (, , ,

+{"id":"bd-1pb","title":"Perf: Baseline + optimization plan (alien-grade rigor)","description":"# Goal
Establish a **profile-driven optimization loop** for the PiJS runtime and connectors with reproducible outputs.

# Method (Extreme Optimization)
1) Baseline: `hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10` on key extension workloads.
2) Profile: `cargo flamegraph` for CPU, heaptrack for allocations.
3) Prove: golden outputs + isomorphism checklist for every change.
4) Implement: only opportunities with score ≥ 2.0.
5) Verify: sha256 of golden outputs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Benchmark scripts for representative extensions.
- Opportunity matrix with impact/confidence/effort.
- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.
- Log artifacts per run (JSONL + summary table).

# Output
- Short performance report tied to `bd-btq` success criteria.","notes":"Progress (2026-02-03): Added Criterion microbench group ext_js_runtime for QuickJsRuntime (cold start, warm eval, run_pending_jobs empty) in benches/extensions.rs. Documented baseline numbers + repeatable loop + opportunity matrix template in BENCHMARKS.md. Gates green: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test.  {"id":"bd-1pf","title":"Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming","description":"# Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming

## Goal
Create infrastructure to record and replay real API responses for provider testing.

## Background
VCR (Video Cassette Recorder) testing pattern:
1. First run: Make real API call, record response to file
2. Subsequent runs: Replay recorded response, no network

This gives us:
- Real response format testing (not simplified mocks)
- Deterministic CI (no flaky network tests)
- Real error response testing (rate limits, auth failures)

## Implementation

### Recording Mode
```rust
// tests/vcr/mod.rs

pub struct VcrRecorder {
    cassette_path: PathBuf,
    mode: VcrMode,
}

pub enum VcrMode {
    Record,    // Make real calls, save to cassette
    Playback,  // Replay from cassette, fail if not found
    Auto,      // Playback if exists, else record
}

impl VcrRecorder {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }

    pub async fn request(&self, req: Request) -> Response {
        match self.mode {
            VcrMode::Record => self.record_and_return(req).await,
            VcrMode::Playback => self.playback(req),
            VcrMode::Auto => ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Cassette Format
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "test_name": "anthropic_streaming_text",
  "recorded_at": "2026-02-02T...",
  "interactions": [
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        "headers": { ... },
        "body": { ... }
      },
      "response": {
        "status": 200,
        "headers": { ... },
        "body_chunks": [
          "event: message_start\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          "event: content_block_delta\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          ...
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### SSE Streaming Support
For streaming responses, store individual chunks:
- Each SSE event as separate chunk
- Preserve timing information (optional replay delay)
- Support partial responses (stream interruption)

### Sensitive Data Redaction
```rust
pub fn redact_cassette(cassette: &mut Cassette) {
    // Replace API keys with placeholder
    cassette.redact_header("x-api-key", "[REDACTED]");
    cassette.redact_header("authorization", "[REDACTED]");

    // Replace sensitive response data
    cassette.redact_body_field("api_key", "[REDACTED]");
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log record/playback actions.
- Log cassette path, mode, request summary (method/url), and redaction summary.
- On playback mismatch, log expected vs actual interaction keys + diff hints.

## Files to Create
- tests/vcr/mod.rs - VCR infrastructure
- tests/vcr/cassette.rs - Cassette format/loading
- tests/vcr/recorder.rs - Recording logic
- tests/vcr/playback.rs - Playback logic
- tests/fixtures/vcr/ - Directory for cassette files

## Testing the VCR System
- Unit test: Load cassette from JSON
- Unit test: Match request to interaction
- Unit test: Redaction works
- Integration: Record + playback cycle

## Environment Variables
- VCR_MODE=record|playback|auto
- VCR_CASSETTE_DIR=path/to/cassettes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VCR infrastructure compiles
- [ ] Recording mode saves cassettes
- [ ] Playback mode replays cassettes
- [ ] SSE streaming supported
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Record/playback actions logged with detail
- [ ] CI uses playback mode only

 {"id":"bd-1rfa","title":"Editor: Shift+Enter newline, Enter submit (legacy multiline)","description":"# Goal
Match legacy multi-line behavior:
- **Shift+Enter** inserts a newline.
- **Enter** submits.
- **Windows Terminal**: **Ctrl+Enter** inserts newline.

# Current Gap
`src/interactive.rs` currently uses different semantics (Alt+Enter mentioned for multi-line mode).

# Implementation Notes
- Ensure this interacts correctly with message queue semantics:
  - When busy, Enter should enqueue steering rather than submit; newline insertion must still be available.
- Preserve standard text editing behavior (cursor movement, deletion).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shift+Enter (and Ctrl+Enter on Windows) inserts newline.
- [ ] Enter submits when idle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:44.567700808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:19:29.557243878Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:19:29.557159039Z","close_reason":"Completed: Shift+Enter/Ctrl+Enter newline; Enter submits (queues when busy)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rfa","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:44.574346662Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rfa","depends_on_id":"bd-340x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:41.787957876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rfa","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:41.222753615Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
